title: Icon Maker
shortDescription: Turn one image into app icons or square brand icons with size, padding, and background controls.
metaDescription: Turn one source image into app icons, launcher icons, or square brand assets with browser-side export.
intro: Make a single polished icon from one image by choosing the output size, padding, background, and file format, then download it straight from your browser.
overview:
This tool is designed for a focused icon-export workflow when you need one clean square asset instead of a full icon set. It works well for app prototypes, launcher tiles, PWA assets, product demos, and profile graphics that need consistent framing.

Start with a logo, symbol, or product mark, then adjust spacing so the subject sits correctly inside the canvas. You can use a solid background for a bold badge-style look or keep transparency when the icon needs to sit on different surfaces.

The result is a practical production file rather than a mockup. That makes it useful for design handoff, early app testing, marketplace previews, and any case where you want a reusable square icon with predictable proportions.
howItWorks: Upload the image you want to convert into an icon.
Choose the canvas size, padding, background color, and output format.
Preview the result, then download the rendered icon file.

useCases.title: App launch tile
useCases.description: Create a centered square icon from a logo file for a mobile app preview, internal build, or store mockup.

useCases.title: PWA home-screen asset
useCases.description: Generate a clean mark with tuned padding so it reads well on a device home screen or launcher grid.

useCases.title: Product avatar icon
useCases.description: Turn a brand symbol into a square profile asset for dashboards, admin panels, or vendor listings.

useCases.title: Badge-style brand mark
useCases.description: Place a simple mark on a solid background to get a compact icon for presentations and pitch decks.

examples.title: Transparent logo to app icon
examples.input: A transparent SVG logo with a wide wordmark removed
examples.output: A 512×512 PNG icon with centered symbol and 18% padding
examples.description: Good for app testing when you need a simple square asset that keeps the logo readable.

examples.title: Monogram to dark badge
examples.input: A two-letter monogram on transparent background
examples.output: A 1024×1024 icon with deep navy background and white mark
examples.description: Useful for a branded avatar or launcher tile that needs stronger contrast.

examples.title: Product symbol to marketplace icon
examples.input: A small product glyph exported from a design file
examples.output: A 256×256 WebP icon with extra padding and transparent background
examples.description: Helps when the mark must sit cleanly on varying surfaces without a fixed frame.

faq.question: What should I upload for the cleanest result?
faq.answer: Use a logo, symbol, or monogram with clear edges and enough empty space around the subject. Very detailed illustrations can lose clarity when scaled down.

faq.question: Why does padding matter so much?
faq.answer: Padding controls how much breathing room the subject has inside the square. Too little makes the icon feel cramped; too much makes it look small and weak.

faq.question: Can I keep the background transparent?
faq.answer: Yes. Transparent output is best when the icon will be placed on different interface colors or inside another design system.

faq.question: What happens if my source image is rectangular?
faq.answer: The tool fits it into a square canvas. If the subject is wide, you may need to reduce padding or use a simpler crop-ready version of the image.

faq.question: Which format should I choose?
faq.answer: PNG is the safest choice for crisp edges and transparency. WebP can be smaller, while a solid background may be better for badge-style icons.
